headline: Centre Approves Large-Scale Procurement of Pulses and Oilseeds Under PSS in Four States; Farmers to Benefit: Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan
fullText:
Ministry of Agriculture &

Centre Approves Large-Scale Procurement of Pulses and Oilseeds Under PSS in Four States; Farmers to Benefit: Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan

Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan approves major MSP procurement of pulses; Uttar Pradesh package worth over ₹1,490 crore

18,250 MT of moong procurement approved for Gujarat with MSP value exceeding ₹160 crore

Tamil Nadu's moong procurement target increased to 990 MT; farmers to receive direct benefits

Approval for procurement of 2,115 MT of moong in Haryana to provide MSP support to farmers

18 JUN 2026 9:08PM by PIB Delhi

Union Minister of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare and Rural Development Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan on Thursday approved large-scale procurement of pulses and oilseeds at Minimum Support Price (MSP) for four states through video conferencing. The procurement will be undertaken under the Price Support Scheme (PSS) in Tamil Nadu, Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh and Haryana, ensuring remunerative prices for farmers and protecting them from distress sales.

Under the decision taken by Union Agriculture Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan, Uttar Pradesh has emerged as the biggest beneficiary. For the Summer 2026 season, procurement of 48,298 metric tonnes (MT) of moong, 97,970 MT of urad and 41,718 MT of groundnut has been approved for the state. The total MSP value of these approvals exceeds ₹1,490 crore. The decision is expected to provide significant support to pulse and oilseed growers in the state.

Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan has also approved procurement of 18,250 MT of moong in Gujarat for the Summer 2026 season. The procurement will be carried out under the PSS and will have a total MSP value of over ₹160 crore. The decision will help moong farmers in the state secure better prices in the market.

In the case of Tamil Nadu, the Union Minister has enhanced the moong procurement limit for the Rabi Marketing Season (RMS) 2025-26 from 885 MT to 990 MT. Accordingly, an additional 105 MT of moong will be procured. The total MSP value of the approved procurement stands at ₹8.68 crore, directly benefiting farmers in the state.

Similarly, for Haryana, approval has been granted for procurement of 2,115 MT of moong during the Summer 2026 season. The total MSP value of the procurement will exceed ₹18 crore. The move is expected to play an important role in providing price support to farmers in the state.

The Centre's decision is being viewed as a significant step in the interest of pulse and oilseed farmers, as it will ensure the benefits of MSP and provide relief from market price pressures.

(Release ID: 2274919)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The Ministry of Agriculture approved large-scale procurement of pulses and oilseeds at Minimum Support Price (MSP) in Tamil Nadu, Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, and Haryana under the Price Support Scheme (PSS) to ensure remunerative prices for farmers and mitigate market volatility risks.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Uttar Pradesh**: 48,298 MT moong, 97,970 MT urad, and 41,718 MT groundnut procurement approved for Summer 2026 season; MSP value exceeding ₹1,490 crore.  
- **Gujarat**: 18,250 MT moong procurement for Summer 2026 season approved under PSS; MSP value exceeding ₹160 crore.  
- **Tamil Nadu**: Moong procurement limit increased from 885 MT to 990 MT (additional 105 MT) for RMS 2025-26; MSP value ₹8.68 crore.  
- **Haryana**: 2,115 MT moong procurement approved for Summer 2026 season; MSP value exceeding ₹18 crore.  
- Total moong procurement across all four states approved: 78,713 MT (48,298 + 18,250 + 990 + 2,115) MT.  
- Decisions made through video conferencing by Union Agriculture Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan.  
- Measures aim to provide price stability and direct benefits to farmers in the four participating states.

headline: International Festival of Yoga and Meditation 2026 Celebrated in Leh
fullText:
International Festival of Yoga and Meditation 2026 Celebrated in Leh

Seminar on ‘Integration of Yoga for Public Health’ Held at National Institute of Sowa Rigpa

Experts Highlight Role of Yoga and Sowa Rigpa in Promoting Holistic Health and Well-being

18 JUN 2026 9:03PM by PIB Delhi

As part of the International Festival of Yoga and Meditation 2026, the National Institute of Sowa Rigpa (NISR), Leh, in collaboration with Mahabodhi International Meditation Centre, Leh, organized a seminar on “Integration of Yoga for Public Health.” The seminar brought together experts, practitioners, delegates and participants to deliberate on the role of Yoga and traditional systems of medicine in promoting holistic health and well-being.

The programme began with a visit to the institute and its museum, followed by seminar proceedings. Addressing the gathering, Dr. Padma Gurmet, Director, NISR, highlighted the significance of the festival and emphasized the integration of Yoga with Sowa Rigpa for strengthening preventive healthcare and public health initiatives.

Chief Guest Dr. Ishwar V. Basavaraddi, Former Director, Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga, shared his views on the role of Yoga in holistic health and well-being. Ven. Bhikkhu Sanghasena spoke about meditation, compassion and inner well-being, underlining their importance in fostering sustainable health and social harmony.

The seminar featured presentations on “Building Emotional Resilience through Yoga,” “Emotional Fitness: A Skill for the Future,” and "The integration of Yoga with Sowa Rigpa", highlighting various dimensions of Yoga in promoting physical, emotional and holistic well-being.

The seminar was organized as part of the week-long International Festival of Yoga and Meditation 2026 being held across Ladakh from 15–21 June to commemorate the 12th UN International Day of Yoga. The festival includes Yogathon, meditation sessions, youth outreach programmes and Yoga events at iconic locations across Ladakh.

Key highlights of the festival include special Yoga sessions at Siachen Base Camp, one of the world’s highest military deployments, and Pangong Lake, reflecting the universal message of Yoga and showcasing Ladakh as a unique destination for wellness, meditation and holistic living.

(Release ID: 2274909)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
